As a member of the Urticaceae family, it shares similarities with other plants such as stinging nettles and figworts. intro: Dahlstedtia araripensis, also known as the Araripina vine, is a plant species native to the northeastern region of Brazil, characterized by its woody vine habit and small, yellowish-green flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and fever, due to its purported health benefits. Research has identified several bioactive constituents in D. araripensis, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, D. araripensis is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in oil to make a topical treatment for skin irritations and wounds.
